query:
Noticed interesting behavior in the bugfix.. 4 requests in a row:  unimatrix - context_store (MCP)(title: "Default freshness half-life (168h) miscalibrated for durable knowledge bases — silently kills ADR and convention confidence", content:
                                    "FRESHNESS_HALF_LIFE_HOURS=168.0 (1 week) was set for ephemeral/transient content. For a durable knowledge base holding ADRs and architectural decisions, a
                                    26-day-old entry scores exp(-3.71) ≈ 0.024 — effectively dead — despite being the governing answer. Root cause: compiled constant magnitude was not validated
                                    against actual domain content lifetime. Missing guard: a semantic regression test asserting freshness_score for a 30-day-old entry under the default
                                    collaborative preset remains above a meaningful floor (e.g., 0.1). Such a test would have made the miscalibration visible before shipping. Takeaway: when a
                                    compiled constant backs a configurable default, validate the constant against the domain's expected content lifetime, not a convenient round number.", topic:
                                    "unimatrix-engine", category: "lesson-learned", tags: "[\"bugfix\", \"confidence\", \"freshness\", \"half-life\", \"calibration\", \"default-value\",
                                    \"caused_by_feature:dsn-001\"]", agent_id: "426-investigator")
     unimatrix - context_store (MCP)(title: "Default freshness half-life (168h) miscalibrated for durable knowledge bases — silently kills ADR and convention confidence", content:
                                    "FRESHNESS_HALF_LIFE_HOURS=168.0 (1 week) was set for ephemeral/transient content. For a durable knowledge base holding ADRs and architectural decisions, a
                                    26-day-old entry scores exp(-3.71) ≈ 0.024 — effectively dead — despite being the governing answer. Root cause: compiled constant magnitude was not validated
                                    against actual domain content lifetime. Missing guard: a semantic regression test asserting freshness_score for a 30-day-old entry under the default
                                    collaborative preset remains above a meaningful floor (e.g., 0.1). Such a test would have made the miscalibration visible before shipping. Takeaway: when a
                                    compiled constant backs a configurable default, validate the constant against the domain's expected content lifetime, not a convenient round number.", topic:
                                    "unimatrix-engine", category: "lesson-learned", tags: "[\"bugfix\", \"confidence\", \"freshness\", \"half-life\", \"calibration\", \"default-value\",
                                    \"caused_by_feature:dsn-001\"]", agent_id: "426-investigator")
     unimatrix - context_store (MCP)(title: "Default freshness half-life (168h) miscalibrated for durable knowledge bases — silently kills ADR and convention confidence", content:
                                    "FRESHNESS_HALF_LIFE_HOURS=168.0 (1 week) was set for ephemeral/transient content. For a durable knowledge base holding ADRs and architectural decisions, a
                                    26-day-old entry scores exp(-3.71) ≈ 0.024 — effectively dead — despite being the governing answer. Root cause: compiled constant magnitude was not validated
                                    against actual domain content lifetime. Missing guard: a semantic regression test asserting freshness_score for a 30-day-old entry under the default
                                    collaborative preset remains above a meaningful floor (e.g., 0.1). Such a test would have made the miscalibration visible before shipping. Takeaway: when a
                                    compiled constant backs a configurable default, validate the constant against the domain's expected content lifetime, not a convenient round number.", topic:

                                    "[\"bugfix\",\"confidence\",\"freshness\",\"half-life\",\"calibration\",\"default-value\",\"caused_by_feature:dsn-001\"]", agent_id: "426-investigator")
     unimatrix - context_store (MCP)(title: "Default freshness half-life (168h) miscalibrated for durable knowledge bases — silently kills ADR and convention confidence", content:

                                    26-day-old entry scores exp(-3.71) ≈ 0.024 — effectively dead — despite being the governing answer. Root cause: compiled constant magnitude was not validated

                                    collaborative preset remains above a meaningful floor (e.g., 0.1). Such a test would have made the miscalibration visible before shipping. Takeaway: when a

entries.title: Default freshness half-life (168h) miscalibrated for durable knowledge bases — silently kills ADR and convention confidence
Decouple freshness-decay tests from ConfidenceParams::default() when changing FRESHNESS_HALF_LIFE_HOURS
ADR-005 col-020b: Time-Boxed #193 Investigation with Store-Layer Scope Boundary
Verify reported bug scope is still live before diagnosing: gates can be silently removed and components can be removed from pipelines
MCP Tool Permission Errors on context_store Block Knowledge Stewardship
